title: Persist settings locally, auto-reconnect printer, and duplicate serial error

requirements.summary: Persist all label settings to localStorage using Zustand persist middleware and automatically rehydrate on app open
acceptanceCriteria: Label settings changed by the user are saved to localStorage immediately.
On app open/reload, labelSettingsStore rehydrates from localStorage and the UI reflects the saved values.
If no saved settings exist, defaults are applied as before.

file_operations.description: Add Zustand persist middleware to the labelSettingsStore to automatically save all state (including barcode positions, dimensions, font sizes, layout settings, and prefix mappings) to localStorage and rehydrate on app initialization. Configure the persist middleware with a storage key and ensure all fields in the store state are included in persistence.

requirements.summary: Persist prefix-to-label-type mappings to localStorage as part of labelSettingsStore persistence
acceptanceCriteria: Prefix mappings configured by the user survive a page reload.
On app open, the prefix mappings are restored from localStorage and rendered correctly in the Label Settings tab.
New prefix mappings added by the user are persisted immediately.

file_operations.description: Verify that the prefixMappings field in labelSettingsStore is included in the persisted state and not excluded from the persist middleware configuration. Ensure prefixMappings updates trigger immediate persistence to localStorage.

requirements.summary: Save connected printer identifiers to localStorage and auto-reconnect on app open
acceptanceCriteria: After a successful USB printer connection, the printer's identifying details are saved to localStorage.
On app open, if saved printer details exist, a reconnection attempt is made automatically without user interaction.
If reconnection succeeds, the printer status badge shows connected.
If reconnection fails (device not available), a non-blocking error is logged and the user can manually connect from the Devices tab.
If no prior printer was ever connected, no auto-connect attempt is made.

file_operations.description: After successful USB printer connection via connectPrinter, extract the printer's USB vendor ID, product ID, and serial number (if available) from the WebUSB device object and save them to localStorage under a key like 'lastConnectedPrinter'. Add a new action autoReconnectPrinter that reads the saved printer identifiers from localStorage on app initialization, attempts to reconnect using the usbCpclTransport requestDevice with filters matching the saved IDs, and updates the connection state. If reconnection fails, log a non-blocking error to logStore without throwing. Wire this auto-reconnect action to be called from a useEffect in a top-level component.

Add a new exported function reconnectToDevice that accepts USB vendor ID, product ID, and optional serial number, then uses navigator.usb.getDevices() to find a matching previously authorized device. If found, open the device and return the UsbDevice object; if not found or connection fails, return null. This enables silent reconnection without triggering a new browser permission prompt.

Import the printerService store and call the autoReconnectPrinter action in a useEffect hook that runs once on mount (after password session unlock). Ensure the auto-reconnect attempt only runs when the app is unlocked and does not block rendering or login flow.

requirements.summary: Detect duplicate serial numbers in two-step scan workflow, display inline error, play error sound, and block printing
acceptanceCriteria: When the 2nd scanned serial is exactly the same as the 1st (after normalization), an error message is shown inline in the scan workflow (e.g., 'Serial numbers must be unique — please scan a different serial number.').
The error sound from soundSystem.ts is triggered immediately when the duplicate is detected.
The print action is blocked; no CPCL is generated or sent to the printer.
The 2nd serial input is cleared so the user can scan again.
The 1st serial remains set so the user does not need to re-scan it.
When the 2nd serial is different from the 1st, the workflow proceeds normally.

file_operations.description: In the two-step scanning workflow (after the 2nd serial is scanned), normalize both the 1st and 2nd serial values using serialNormalization.ts normalizeSerial function, then compare them for exact equality. If they match, set a local error state with the message 'Serial numbers must be unique — please scan a different serial number.', call soundSystem playErrorSound action, clear the 2nd serial input field, and prevent the print workflow from proceeding. Display the error message inline near the 2nd serial input using an Alert or error text component. When the 2nd serial is changed or cleared, reset the error state. Ensure the 1st serial remains intact so the user can immediately re-scan the 2nd serial.
